frame_analyses.description: Initial setup with needle drivers visible, yellow Penrose drain with two marks present
Right needle driver grasping needle, preparing for suture placement
Needle being driven through first mark on Penrose drain
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Entry appears close to mark, good angle of approach

frame_analyses.description: Needle passing through second mark, completing suture placement
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Both entry and exit points appear well-positioned

frame_analyses.description: Beginning formation of first throw (surgeon's knot - double throw)
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Right hand leading the knot formation

frame_analyses.description: Completing first throw with double wrap visible
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good double throw formation, appropriate tension

frame_analyses.description: Beginning second throw, hands have switched positions
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Left hand now leading, proper hand alternation

frame_analyses.description: Completing second throw, single wrap formation
Beginning third throw, right hand leading again
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper alternation maintained, right hand leading third throw

frame_analyses.description: Completing final throw and tightening knot
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final throw completed, knot appears secure
Both tails being cut inside the trainer as required

penalties.description: Minor deviation from marks estimated at approximately 1-2mm total

knot_assessments.notes: Clean double throw formation, appropriate tension
Proper hand alternation, clean single throw
Final throw completed with proper alternation

confidence_rationale: Clear visualization of knot formation sequence and hand alternation. Suture placement appears accurate with minimal deviation. Camera angle provides good view of technique throughout.
cannot_determine: Exact millimeter measurements of suture deviation due to video resolution and angle
technique_summary: Strong intermediate to expert level performance with excellent knot technique, proper hand alternation, and efficient timing. All required elements completed correctly.
strengths: Proper surgeon's knot formation with double throw
Consistent hand alternation between all throws
Secure knot formation with appropriate tension
improvement_suggestions: Aim for more precise needle placement to minimize deviation from marks
Could potentially improve speed in third throw phase
